title: HumanUniqueness nullifier is constant across sessions, enabling cross-verifier linkability
description: The HumanUniqueness nullifier is `Poseidon2(scope, secret)` where scope is a constant like `hash('bolyra-handshake-v1')`. This means every handshake by the same human produces the identical nullifier, allowing colluding verifiers (or a single verifier across sessions) to link all activity to the same pseudonymous identity. The nonceBinding output prevents replay but does NOT prevent linkability. Fix: derive the nullifier as `Poseidon3(scope, secret, sessionNonce)` so each session produces a unique nullifier. Then use a separate `externalNullifier = Poseidon2(scope, secret)` checked on-chain only for revocation/sybil gating, not exposed to the verifier. This preserves sybil resistance while breaking cross-session linkability.
